summary: Iteration 5 (multi-dataset registry) verified end-to-end. Backend: 53/53 pytest pass — 18 iter1 regression + 7 iter3 (legacy /open-dataset/*) + 10 iter4 (admin token guard) + 18 NEW iter5 covering the new /api/admin/datasets/* registry. Frontend: TeacherActions dialog opens with 3 dataset tabs (UCI 30/0, OULAD 25/0, KAGGLE_XAPI 25/0); OULAD import succeeded with toast '+25 students, +350 attempts', tab counter flipped to 25/25, snapshot KPI updated to '35 STUDENTS TRACKED', and at-risk panel populated with 'OU Learner #...' entries. reset-all wiped imports and restored counters to 0/25 baseline. Dataset state restored to baseline (10 seeded students) at end of run.

verification_results.list_datasets_endpoint: PASS — GET /api/admin/datasets returns 3 entries (uci, oulad, kaggle_xapi) with name/source/available_records/already_imported. Counts: 30/25/25.
verification_results.info_per_dataset: PASS — GET /api/admin/datasets/{key}/info works for all 3 keys with same shape.
verification_results.import_oulad: PASS — 25 students + 350 attempts (14/student = 4 primary + 2*5 other), source=dataset:oulad.
verification_results.idempotency: PASS — second import returns imported_students=0, already_existed=N for each dataset.
verification_results.reset_isolation: PASS — after importing all 3, POST /admin/datasets/oulad/reset removes ONLY oulad; uci stays at 30 and kaggle_xapi stays at 25.
verification_results.reset_all: PASS — POST /admin/datasets/reset-all returns deleted_students/deleted_attempts/per_dataset breakdown; all counts back to 0.
verification_results.admin_token_guard: PASS — all 5 new admin endpoints (datasets list, info, import, reset, reset-all) return 401 'Invalid admin token' without header or with wrong token; 200 with X-Admin-Token=deis-demo-admin-2026.
verification_results.legacy_aliases: PASS — GET/POST /api/admin/open-dataset/{info,import,reset} still work and proxy to UCI; importing via legacy path is reflected in /admin/datasets/uci/info.
verification_results.downstream_after_full_import: PASS — /api/students returns 90 (10 + 30 + 25 + 25); /api/teacher/class-overview 200; drill-down on oulad_<id> student via /api/students/{id} returns populated dashboard with subject/weak data.
verification_results.iter1_to_iter4_regression: PASS — every endpoint in backend_test.py + test_iteration3.py + test_iteration4.py still green (35/35).
verification_results.frontend_dialog_opens: PASS — clicking import-dataset-btn opens import-dialog showing 3 tabs (dataset-tab-uci, -oulad, -kaggle_xapi) with mono '0/30 0/25 0/25' counts.
verification_results.frontend_tab_switching: PASS — clicking each tab swaps dataset-panel-{key}; description, available, already-imported tiles update.
verification_results.frontend_per_key_import: PASS — confirm-import-btn-oulad fired POST /api/admin/datasets/oulad/import with X-Admin-Token; toast 'Open University Learning Analytics (OULAD) sample: +25 students, +350 attempts' shown; tab counter flipped to 25/25; KPI 'STUDENTS TRACKED' went 10 -> 35.
verification_results.frontend_reset_all: PASS — reset-all-btn fires reset-all; OULAD tab back to 0/25; baseline restored.
verification_results.frontend_admin_header: PASS — TeacherActions.jsx uses ADMIN_HEADERS (X-Admin-Token from REACT_APP_ADMIN_TOKEN) on every /admin/* call (list, info via refresh, import, reset, reset-all).

critical_code_review_comments: (GOOD) core/datasets.py is a clean single-file registry (~350 lines): DatasetSpec dataclass + 3 pure transformer functions + 5 public async helpers. New datasets can be added with one JSON file + transformer + 1 line in DATASETS dict.
(GOOD) `source` tag scheme `dataset:<key>` makes per-dataset reset trivial and avoids ever touching seeded stu_001..stu_010.
(GOOD) reset_all_datasets() also cleans the legacy 'uci_open_dataset' tag — backwards-safe migration.
(GOOD) routers/admin.py is tiny (~60 lines) — all logic pushed down into core/datasets.py. Legacy aliases simply call `ds.dataset_info('uci')` etc., zero duplication.

(MINOR) DatasetSpec.transform receives `now` but each transformer immediately calls datetime.now()-ish via timedelta off `now`; consistent. However _mk_attempt() uses datetime.now(timezone.utc) directly for completed_at — small inconsistency, not a bug.
(MINOR) import_dataset() loops one row at a time and only batches inserts at the end; for 25–30 row datasets fine, but if scale grows consider chunked insert_many.
(MINOR) HTTPException is imported in core/datasets.py — couples a 'core' module to FastAPI. Acceptable but a stricter layering would raise a custom DatasetError caught by the router.

(MINOR) TeacherActions.jsx: the disabled state on reset-dataset-btn-{key} is `selected.already_imported === 0` — good UX but means the data-testid can be enabled/disabled depending on state; tests must import first before clicking remove.

(MINOR) `data-testid={selected ? `confirm-import-btn-${selected.key}` : 'confirm-import-btn'}` fallback is good defensive coding for the loading frame, but only the suffixed form is documented in the spec.
(MINOR) startup/shutdown deprecation in FastAPI 0.110+ (lifespan context) — carry-over from prior iterations, still not fixed.
(MINOR) REACT_APP_ADMIN_TOKEN is bundled into the JS at build time — public; acceptable for demo. Already documented in security.py.
test_credentials: X-Admin-Token: deis-demo-admin-2026 (env: backend ADMIN_TOKEN, frontend REACT_APP_ADMIN_TOKEN). Dataset keys: uci, oulad, kaggle_xapi. No user auth.
seed_data_creation: All datasets reset to baseline (10 seeded students, 0 imports across all 3 datasets) at end of run via /api/admin/datasets/reset-all.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration5.py is the canonical regression for the multi-dataset registry. It auto-resets before/after the module so it is safe to re-run alongside iter3/iter4. Iter3 still exercises the legacy /open-dataset/* path; iter4 still exercises auth on those legacy paths; iter5 exercises both the new /datasets/* path and the legacy aliases. If new datasets are added, simply extend DATASET_KEYS / EXPECTED_AVAILABLE / EXPECTED_ATTEMPTS_PER_STUDENT at the top of iter5. The /api root (GET /api/) is the closest thing to a health endpoint — there is no /api/health.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found this iteration. One self-fix during testing: my initial iter5 test pinged /api/health which doesn't exist; I switched it to GET /api/ which is the actual health-check endpoint.
